name: The Nostalgia Returner
description: Goes back to the same 4-5 places year after year. Has 'their' table at a restaurant in Provence, 'their' room at a hotel in the Lake District. Deeply resistant to novelty. Values Atmosphere and Character through the lens of familiarity and accumulated memory. Tests whether the system can handle someone who doesn't want discovery — the entire Discover feed premise is challenged.

contradictions.stated: Doesn't need new places — has everything they want
contradictions.revealed: Actually deeply curious about one very specific type of new experience: places that remind them of their favorites. Would try a new restaurant if someone said 'it's like that bistro you love in Lyon'
contradictions.expectedResolution: Properties with strong atmosphere similarity to their known favorites — not discovery for novelty, but discovery through resonance

voiceAnswerGuidance.memorable-stays: Tells long stories about the SAME places visited many times. 'We've been going to this hotel in Positano for eleven years. The owner knows our kids' names. We always get the room on the third floor with the lemon tree outside.' Every memory is layered with previous visits.
voiceAnswerGuidance.anti-stay: Anywhere that tries too hard to be new or trendy. 'We tried this new place in Barcelona everyone was raving about. It felt like a stage set. No soul. We went back to our usual place the next night.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.adaptive-conversation: Every answer circles back to their known places. If food: 'There's a bistro in Lyon where the patron has been serving the same menu for 30 years. The duck is exactly the same every time. That's what I want from food.' If design: 'I like places that look like they've been lived in. Worn leather, faded curtains, a crack in the tile someone decided to keep.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.details-matter: Notices continuity and change. 'I track what's different each year. Did they repaint the shutters? Is the olive tree bigger? Is the same family running the kitchen? These small changes against the backdrop of sameness — that's what matters to me.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.emotional-core: Travels to return to a feeling. Continuity, memory, belonging. MEMORY_KEEPER.
